headline: Models nail tech rally while Apple disappoints across the board
summary: AI models showed strong consensus on SPY and QQQ upside, correctly predicting the market's bullish momentum with tech stocks leading the charge. However, all four models that predicted Apple would rise were wrong, as AAPL closed at $267.61 despite widespread optimism about upcoming earnings.

consensus_picks.note: Four models correctly called SPY's rise to $715.17, while only Claude dissented with a bearish view.
All three models targeting QQQ nailed the tech-heavy index's climb to $664.23.
A rare unanimous miss as all four models expected Apple to rise ahead of earnings, but it fell to $267.61.
Both models correctly identified NVDA's continued strength, with the stock reaching $216.61.
Both bearish calls on Tesla were wrong as the stock surged to $378.67, defying production delay concerns.

best_call.summary: Perplexity's QQQ call earned the day's highest score, correctly identifying tech momentum with strong 65% confidence.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence Apple bull call backfired the most, expecting $275 but getting $267.61 instead.
